title: Remove explicit cast('list[Row]') in test_dns_instrument once query_db overloads land — address in #1170 (type-checker migration)
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

PR #1136 fixes mypy on the dynamic-port dns tests with explicit `cast("list[Row]", db_utils.query_db(...))` band-aids. Maintainer dislikes these and wants the root fix landed in PR #1154 (not by re-churning the converged #1136 trunk).

Locations (on refactor/test-dynamic-ports @ 03b5a49e):
- test/test_dns_instrument.py:2  (`from typing import cast` import)
- test/test_dns_instrument.py:24, :79, :121  (the cast call sites)

Root cause: `openwpm/utilities/db_utils.py::query_db` returns `List[Union[sqlite3.Row, Tuple]]`, so string-key indexing on the un-narrowed union fails type-checking, forcing the casts.

Proper fix (reference impl already exists on fix/dynamic-test-ports-v2 / #1153):
- Add `@overload`s to `query_db`: `as_tuple=False` (default) -> `List[sqlite3.Row]`; `as_tuple=True` -> `List[Tuple]`. Do the same for `get_javascript_entries`.
- Then delete the casts and the `from typing import cast` import in test_dns_instrument.py (and any other call sites that no longer need them).

Sequencing: removing #1136's casts requires #1154 to be stacked on #1136 (the casts live in #1136's file). So this lands as part of #1154 once #1154 is rebased onto #1136. The `query_db` overload itself is shared infra (db_utils.py) and is independent of the stealth-vector decisions blocking the rest of #1154.

Verification: mypy clean with no `cast` import in test_dns_instrument.py; the dns tests still pass.
